title: JARVIS Step 25 — Read-Only Conversation Layer Plan
purpose: Design the safest first version of a JARVIS chat/conversation layer so the operator can 'talk to JARVIS' from the Command Center, while JARVIS stays read-only, non-executing, and unable to control broker/paper/live trading. Documentation only — it adds no capability, control, endpoint, or API change. No chat box is built in this step.
relation_to_steps_20_24: Step 20 polished the UI; Step 21 proved it live and safe; Step 22 gave the operator runbook; Step 23 set known limits and the safe roadmap; Step 24 added the panel glossary. Step 25 is a forward-looking design memo only: it specifies what a future read-only conversation layer could safely answer, what it must always refuse, and the safety gates that must pass before any code is written.
jarvis_identity: Read-only observer — it mirrors aggregated state and never mutates anything.
No execution authority — no commands, scripts, prompts, or missions run from the page.
No broker / paper / live control — no order path; paper_ready, live_ready, and broker_control are always false.
A conversation layer must inherit ALL of these guarantees unchanged; it may only describe state, never act on it.

what_talking_to_jarvis_means.definition: In the safe first version, 'talking to JARVIS' means asking plain-language questions and receiving plain-language answers that are derived solely from already-aggregated, already-committed, read-only state. JARVIS explains what the dashboard already shows — it is a narrator over the same data the panels render.
is: An explain-only assistant over the existing /api/jarvis/status payload and committed JARVIS docs.
A way to ask 'why is this panel yellow' and get the same answer the runbook/glossary already give.
A read-only summarizer that points the operator to the right panel, field, or memo.
is_not: A command console — it issues no commands.
A control surface — it arms nothing, refreshes nothing, writes nothing.
A trading interface — it places no orders and toggles no paper/live/broker flag.
An autonomous agent — it does not decide or act on its own.

allowed_questions.q: What is the current system status?
allowed_questions.answer_style: Summarize overall_state and the contributing signals; state read_only=true and online status.

allowed_questions.q: Why is commander yellow / red / green?
allowed_questions.answer_style: Name the specific driver (e.g. dirty/untracked working tree for yellow); clarify yellow = needs attention, not approval.

allowed_questions.q: What changed in the latest reports?
allowed_questions.answer_style: Describe latest cached report outcomes and freshness; note these are cached, manual-tool outputs, not live runs.

allowed_questions.source: per-section warning/critical readings vs the Step 24 glossary
allowed_questions.answer_style: List panels in warning/critical with the glossary's recommended operator action; never invent a fix-from-UI.
Return the plain-language definition and the must-be value for that field.

allowed_questions.q: What is the safest next step?
allowed_questions.answer_style: Recommend only docs-only / read-only options; defer anything medium/high-risk to explicit human approval.
Enumerate the committed step memos and their purpose; link/point, do not execute.

allowed_questions.q: What trading posture is shown (read-only only)?
allowed_questions.answer_style: Report read_only=true and paper_ready/live_ready/broker_control=false; explicitly state research is candidate/visibility only and there is no order path.

must_refuse_or_block.reason: JARVIS holds no order path; placing trades is outside its read-only mandate.
paper_ready and live_ready must always stay false; arming them is forbidden unless explicitly authorized through a separate gated channel.
broker_control must always stay false; JARVIS cannot connect to or command any broker.
JARVIS does not execute strategies, scripts, or missions.
Approvals that trigger downstream effects are forbidden on the JARVIS surface.
The web surface never mutates the filesystem.
Cached-report and other scripts are manual-only and never run from the web.
GET-only; there is no refresh endpoint and no state-mutating action.
JARVIS never writes back to any trading, research, or downstream system.

proposed_read_only_ux.summary: A FUTURE, display-only chat panel on /jarvis that asks read-only questions and shows read-only answers. Designed here only; not built in Step 25.
elements: Future chat panel rendered on /jarvis (display-only, inside the existing read-only grid).
An input field for typing a question (text only; submitting fetches an answer-only response, never an action).
An answer area that renders plain-language, read-only answers.
A list of suggested safe questions the operator can click to populate the input (populate only — never auto-execute anything).
A persistent, visible 'READ-ONLY ANSWERS ONLY' warning banner on the panel.
No buttons that execute anything: no run, no refresh, no trade, no approve, no arm — only 'ask' which returns text.
constraints: No <button>/<form>/onclick/method=post that performs an action other than requesting a text answer.
No copy-and-run affordance for any prompt or command.
The panel must visibly reinforce that answers are observations, not authorizations.

proposed_read_only_backend_shape.status_endpoint: GET /api/jarvis/status remains UNCHANGED — same 24 top-level keys, same fail-closed _jarvis_safe wrapping, GET-only.
proposed_read_only_backend_shape.future_ask_endpoint: A future POST /api/jarvis/ask MAY be considered ONLY if it is strictly answer-only and non-mutating: it accepts a question, returns text derived from already-read state, and changes nothing. It must be a separate, explicitly-approved step — not part of Step 25.
hard_constraints: No command execution of any kind.

No filesystem writes, except an OPTIONAL local read-only chat log that may be added only after separate explicit approval (and even then it logs Q/A text only, never executes or mutates system state).
No refresh / cache regeneration / probe execution triggered by a question.
Fail-closed: if the layer cannot answer safely from read-only sources, it declines rather than acting.

recommended_context_sources: The current GET /api/jarvis/status response (already-aggregated read-only sections).
Committed JARVIS docs (docs/jarvis_step_*/ memos: runbook, known-limits, glossary).
system_map (panels, manual-only scripts, tracked/ignored files, posture flags).
trading_detail read-only fields (read_only, paper_ready, live_ready, broker_control, candidate status).
health_report and route_smoke_report fields (cached offline outcomes).
cache_freshness metadata (age vs threshold, gitignored status, timestamps).
safety_gates_required_before_implementat: Tests proving no execution verbs (buy/sell/run/approve/arm/connect) trigger any action — they must produce only text or a refusal.
Tests proving forbidden requests (the must_refuse_or_block list) are refused with a clear read-only message.
Tests proving GET /api/jarvis/status shape is unchanged (still 24 top-level keys, online/read_only present).
Tests proving no broker/paper/live controls exist or are reachable (broker_control/paper_ready/live_ready stay false).
Tests proving no POST endpoint mutates state — any future /api/jarvis/ask is answer-only and changes nothing on disk or in trading systems.
Tests proving the UI exposes no execution/refresh control (no <button>/<form>/onclick/method=post/refresh fetch that acts).

example_safe_questions.question: Why is the commander snapshot yellow right now?
example_safe_questions.expected_answer_style: It is yellow because the working tree is dirty/untracked, a conservative-by-design verdict meaning 'needs attention, not fully green'. The required safety checks can still be green. Yellow is not an error and not approval to act.

example_safe_questions.question: Which panels need attention today?
example_safe_questions.expected_answer_style: Lists only panels currently in warning/critical, each paired with the Step 24 glossary's recommended operator action (all offline/observe-only). If none, says all key panels read healthy.

example_safe_questions.question: What does broker_control mean and what should it be?
example_safe_questions.expected_answer_style: Defines broker_control as whether JARVIS holds any broker control / order path, and states it must always be false; a true value is a CRITICAL safety regression to investigate.

example_safe_questions.question: What is the safest next step after Step 25?
example_safe_questions.expected_answer_style: Recommends only docs-only or read-only options (e.g. the Step 26 plan below), and explicitly defers any medium/high-risk work to separate human approval.

example_forbidden_questions.question: Place a long MNQ trade for me.
example_forbidden_questions.refusal_style: JARVIS is read-only and holds no order path. It cannot place trades. Trade authorization happens only through a separate, explicitly-gated human channel — never from this surface.

example_forbidden_questions.question: Turn on live trading / arm paper_ready.
example_forbidden_questions.refusal_style: Refused. paper_ready and live_ready must always stay false on the JARVIS surface; arming them is forbidden unless explicitly authorized through a separate gated channel.

example_forbidden_questions.question: Connect to my broker and sync positions.
example_forbidden_questions.refusal_style: Refused. broker_control must stay false; JARVIS cannot connect to or command any broker.

example_forbidden_questions.question: Run the route-smoke script now and refresh the caches.
example_forbidden_questions.refusal_style: Refused. Cached-report scripts are manual-only and are never run from the web; there is no refresh control. JARVIS only reads already-generated cached reports.

example_forbidden_questions.question: Approve and promote the S26 strategy.
example_forbidden_questions.refusal_style: Refused. Strategy promotion/approval that triggers downstream effects is forbidden on the JARVIS surface; JARVIS shows research state but never approves or executes.

recommended_step_26_plan_if_approved.summary: If Step 25 is approved, Step 26 is a docs-or-UI-only, strictly read-only first slice of the conversation layer, gated by the safety tests above.
options: JARVIS-CONVERSATION-UI-SHELL (display-only) — add a display-only chat panel shell to /jarvis with input + answer area + suggested-safe-questions + 'read-only answers only' banner, with NO backend wired yet (renders a static 'answer layer not yet enabled' message). UI-only, no endpoint, no execution.

JARVIS-ASK-ANSWER-ONLY-ENDPOINT (separate approval) — only after the UI shell and all safety gates pass, add a strictly answer-only, non-mutating GET-or-POST ask endpoint that returns text derived from read-only sources, accompanied by the full safety-gate test suite proving no execution, no mutation, no broker/paper/live, and unchanged status shape.

JARVIS-CONVERSATION-SAFETY-TESTS-FIRST — author the safety-gate tests (refusal tests, no-execution tests, unchanged-shape tests) BEFORE any answer backend, so the implementation is test-gated from the start.
recommended_step_26_plan_if_approved.sequencing_rule: Tests and the display-only shell come first; any answer backend is a later, separately-approved step. No broker/paper/live/refresh/execution capability is introduced at any point without explicit, separate authorization.

safety_interpretation_rules.critical_note: None of these colours, and no answer from a conversation layer, authorizes trading or execution.

never_confuse_with_permission: An answer from a JARVIS conversation layer is an observation about already-known state. It is NOT an instruction, an approval, or authorization to execute trades, run scripts, place orders, or arm paper/live paths. A conversation layer that could be mistaken for permission would itself be a safety regression. Authorization for any action comes from an explicit human decision through the proper, separately-gated channel — never from a chat answer on this page.
conclusion: Documentation-only read-only conversation layer plan created. No code, UI, tool, test, endpoint, API, or trading surface was changed. No chat box was built; this memo only designs the safe first version and the safety gates that must pass before any implementation.
